text:
Sivers Semiconductors $SIVE. The SATCOM Wildcard Nobody's Talking About 👇

Everyone's focused on Sivers as a silicon photonics / data center play. Fair. But there's a second engine quietly building underneath and it could be massive.

👉Meet ALLSPACE

They build flat-panel, multi-orbit SATCOM terminals for defense and commercial use, maritime, mobility, fixed sites. Think Starlink-grade connectivity, but hardware-agnostic across LEO, MEO and GEO.

And Sivers is their chip supplier.

Here's where it gets interesting. Each ALLSPACE terminal uses up to 1,800 Sivers chips. At up to $10,000 per terminal, the revenue math scales fast:

- 1,000 units → $10M
- 5,000 units → $50M
- 10,000 units → $100M

Volume ramp expected: H2 2026

For context, Sivers' entire 2024 revenue was ~$30M. A few thousand ALLSPACE terminals could materially move the investment case.

This is what "small volumes, big numbers" actually means in semiconductor land. You don't need mass consumer scale. You need the right customer, the right ASP, and the right timing.

text:
From our $SIVE report last night. 

Remember, we keep our reports conservative. 

Once you layer on pro‑forma net cash from the April raise, in‑place Wireless segment earnings, and legacy CST/InP photonics, total enterprise value steps up to ~SEK 4.16B (≈SEK 13.67/share).

With a further SEK 800–1,400M of unmodeled upside from IRIS², a second LiDAR win, an EU sovereign fab, and Marvell pull‑through.

Which collectively justify the convexity‑adjusted 12‑month EV range that underpins the SEK 41–45 per‑share target.

text:
It’s majority institutions moving $AXTI at these valuations, retail shorts might turn out terribly wrong.

If AXT goes down from export controls the photonics buildout does too. (Eg. AXT -> IQE -> Macom/lite) and there are bigger problems to worry about. 

If you just split out AXT’s 10+ upstream JVs into one merchant Western company it would probably be worth more strategically than 5N. 

Also revenue numbers are misleading since these are TTM contracts, not new ones signed. All the upstream materials like 7n high purity indium has spiked immensely since last year. 

It’s all vertically integrated so there might be a NAND style price hike off limited substrates, but unlike Sumitomo, AXT does the entire supply chain minus a few like red phosphorus. 

So it’s more dangerous than you think.

- $QQQ 21.6% in 23 sessions followed by the $SPY 15.2% and the $IWM at 17%
- $XLK continues to be the market dominant group. $AAPL very near an all time high with the obvious horses being $INTC $AMD $MU. 
- Photonics work up once again after being quiet for a few weeks. $AAOI $CIEN $LITE $VIAV $LWLG $AXTI. The group is due up next for earnings this upcoming week. 
- Software $IGV is also giving the impression that we may have bottomed. Some names are already leading that group and on Thursday we had a great earnings reaction and follow through from $TWLO. $DOCN $FSLY $RNG $FIVN $BAND $NBIS ones to watch. 
- $XLE was still the leading group for the week and it's hard to write those names off when you look at the structure on many of those stocks. $CRAK $BOAT $OIH a few places to watch over the upcoming weeks. 
- $BTC $ETH stage 4B setups unfolding with $CRCL being the only real place of interest for me for now. 
- Memory names are being lead by 4 incredibly powerful trend that show no real signs of slowing. $SNDK $STX $MU $WDC. 
- We got through major earning reports last week and this week is also an important one. Focus on names gapping up on large volume and watch the for continuation setups. $MXL is a great example. 
- Keep universe lists updated and I will see you all on Monday!

text:
Memory and photonics have already run hard, but Layer 5 (Capacity Operators) has the biggest catch-up left. 🤖

$DGXX, sitting at only $240M micro-cap, looks like one of the cleanest pure-play #GPUaaS rare setups in the entire AI infrastructure stack.

Most peers are still years behind - stuck in permitting, transformer shortages, and grid upgrades.

DGXX has already completed the hard pivot: owns 70 MW approved power + commissioned ARMS 200 modular Blackwell pods at Alabama. This is literally the fastest way to turn power into revenue with scarce live Blackwell GPUaaS capacity.

Balance sheet is rock-solid: zero debt, ~$93M strong liquidity with no forced dilution. 

The stock has gone through classic micro-cap volatility. Now it sits at a clear inflection point.

First anchor GPUaaS deal signed - $19.6M SubQ AI revenue goes live May 15. This proves the model works and derisks everything. Advancing hyperscaler discussions with customers desperate for immediate capacity.

Superstar team with hyperscaler DNA:  Venkat Rangasamy (ex-Oracle AI Factories) as VP AI Infrastructure, @hansvestberg (ex-Verizon CEO) co-founding USDC, @GerardRotonda (ex-CFO Deutsche Bank) on the board. Talent this caliber only joins a microcap when they see something truly special.

The recent #USDC spinoff is brilliant and shareholder-friendly: with subsidiary entity raises dedicated capital for pods & GPUs separately - no dilution or debt for DGXX parent entity. DGXX keeps 55% ownership and the majority upside.

Roadmap with high visibility: 10 MW by Q3 2026 → ~$137–152M ARR + colocation → $11–$30/share (4-9x from here). Full 70 MW Alabama scaling → $7B–$12B+ market cap upside.

It’s not if but how fast - hyperscalers are hungry for immediately available power and capacity, and DGXX already has it. And, this is just the beginning of a multi-year AI capacity supercycle - demand for live power and GPUs will only keep growing.

$DGXX reminds me of the early-stage #IREN/#CIFR, both rerated 5-10x+ from pre-contract lows - the risk/reward looks exceptional from here, and it is still completely under the radar.

This could be us retail’s chance to get ahead before market finally connects the dots 🔥 (institutions flood in and analysts raise guidance.) 

Disclaimer: not financial advice. Always DYOR.

text:
The tools split because the physics spans 4-5 orders of magnitude in wavelength. An RF antenna is centimeters. A photonic waveguide is nanometers. 

Tackling both in one numerical simulation would be computationally nuts, so we got HFSS for RF and Lumerical for photonics.

Deep learning models can learn an embedding over EM fields that isn’t frequency-constrained.  We can tackle RF and photonics with the same model.

That’s actually what we’re building at @arenaphysica

That’s actually what we’re building at @arenaphysica"}}}, "rest_id": "2050700623595610193", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "116", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
How is $KOPN weaved into all of this photonics, AI, datacenters talk?!

Kopin is a direct play on AI data center photonics / optical interconnects: 

Neural I/o repurposes Kopin’s proprietary MicroLED pixels (via their patented bi-directional NeuralDisplay architecture) as ultra-high-speed optical transceivers. It moves data using photons instead of electrons, replacing dense copper wiring and traditional laser-based systems between GPUs, processors, boards, and racks in AI/smart data centers.

This addresses the exact pain points (power, bandwidth, heat, scalability) that drive CPO demand. It promises the same (or better) performance with a fraction of the power per bit and eliminates expensive laser modules—positioning it as a disruptive, lower-power alternative/complement to the laser-heavy CPO stack ($SIVE’s InP DFB laser arrays, $POET’s interposers, etc.).

https://t.co/jZIG8z9CBJ has placed a $15M initial purchase order to fund the demonstration chipset. Kopin owns 19.9% of https://t.co/jZIG8z9CBJ and is the exclusive manufacturer of the Neural I/o chipsets. Kopin emphasizes its U.S.-based MicroLED manufacturing for secure supply chains (a big plus in AI/defense).

Stifel recently reinstated coverage with a Buy and $5.50 target; the stock has shown momentum on the news.

Kopin is entering the same optical interconnect layer for AI infrastructure with a novel MicroLED approach that sidesteps some of the traditional laser challenges. This reinforces another high-upside, U.S.-centric player in the photonics boom for AI data centers + defense. LONG $KOPN $SIVE

text:
Photonics, Optics & Memory stocks saw another massive breakout to new all time highs last week. 

Many of these stocks are up well over 100%+ year to date.

After the recent mag7 earnings reports; I think data centers/compute providers will be the next sector to run big.

$NBIS - $CRWV - $CIFR And $IREN

On the various Mag7 earnings calls, they noted their current bottleneck wasn’t the availability of chips, but the availability of compute & power/energy. 

There are smaller data center co’s like $SNLH $BITF $APLD - but the 4 I mentioned earlier are my favourites. 

Trade idea;
Wait for a breakout above previous highs for an entry, or a re-test of this descending triangle breakout.

Nobody listened to me about;

$AXTI at $26 before it flew 175% 
$AAOI at $80 and it ran 90% (so far)
$MU at $156 and it launched 200%
$SNDK at $250

text:
$IBKR – here’s how to get real-time market data for any international exchange on IBKR

After going deep on photonics bottleneck plays outside the U.S. (thanks Serenity), I kept running into the same problem: no real-time data. Japan, Taiwan, UK, France, Germany, Sweden ($SIVE right?) – all delayed or missing.

You have two options: buy at market price blind, or pay $1-2/month per exchange for live data.

I went with the latter. Here’s how to set it up.

Step-by-step guide:

1.Log into IBKR and go to Settings (top right, your profile icon)
https://t.co/wi6vysjrJb the Settings menu, tap Market Data Subscriptions
https://t.co/ieOXkA2beU’ll see your current subscriptions listed by region – North America, Europe, Asia Pacific
4.Tap Configure (the gear icon in the top right of the Subscriptions panel)
https://t.co/7lNGUKtQOx for the exchange you want – Taiwan Stock Exchange, Japan TSE, LSE, etc.
6.Add it. Most non-U.S. exchanges run $1-2 USD/month.
7.Confirm and you’ll get a “Thank You” screen once the subscription is active

Is it worth it? Depends on your position size.

If real-time data helps you save just 1% on entry, that’s $100-$1,000 back in your pocket depending on how much you’re deploying – which could be several years worth of the monthly fee in a single trade.

For me, it’s a no-brainer.

Step-by-step guide: 1.Log into IBKR and go to Settings (top right, your profile icon) https://t.co/wi6vysjrJb the Settings menu, tap Market Data Subscriptions https://t.co/ieOXkA2beU’ll see your current subscriptions listed by region – North America, Europe, Asia Pacific 4.Tap Configure (the gear icon in the top right of the Subscriptions panel) https://t.co/7lNGUKtQOx for the exchange you want – Taiwan Stock Exchange, Japan TSE, LSE, etc. 6.Add it. If real-time data helps you save just 1% on entry, that’s $100-$1,000 back in your pocket depending on how much you’re deploying – which could be several years worth of the monthly fee in a single trade. text:
RARE EARTH THEME: Setting up Huge 

Rare earth elements are the "vitamins" of the 2026 technology buildout. You only need a small amount, but without them, the high-performance hardware required for AI, photonics, and space infrastructure simply won't function.

They are the PHSYICAL BOTTLENECK for AI, As of 2026, China still refines over 90% of the world's supply. This has created a "stockpiling" environment as the U.S. and E.U. rush to bring non-Chinese refining facilities (like MP Materials in California) online before new defense bans take effect in 2027.

The Time Gap: While a data center can be built in months, opening a new mine and refinery often takes a decade. This mismatch between skyrocketing demand and slow supply growth is the primary reason for the "rerate" we're about to see. 

When a entire group is setting up, you pay attention.

text:
LWLG — a real materials bet wrapped in a momentum stock NFA

Lightwave Logic makes Perkinamine electro-optic polymers that spin-coat onto silicon photonics wafers in a back-end-of-line step  (<100°C) to convert passive silicon waveguides into sub-volt, high-bandwidth Mach-Zehnder modulators. 

The technology’s figure of merit (Vπ·L ≈ 0.1 V·cm) is roughly an order of magnitude better than silicon plasma-dispersion modulators and competitive with thin-film lithium niobate. 

The relevance to AI is direct: 800G/1.6T/3.2T optical interconnects and co-packaged optics need exactly this — low-drive-voltage, 

BEOL-compatible, foundry-integrable modulators.
Commercial reality is far less mature. FY2025 revenue: $237 thousand   (yes, thousand) on a $2.45B market cap (P/S ~10,000x). Net loss $20.3M;  cash $69M  after  the December 2025 $35M offering at $3.10/share.  Management explicitly states no meaningful production revenue until 2027.   

The investor must accept this is a binary 2027 outcome. The bull case rests on GlobalFoundries PDK integration (March 2026), Tower Semiconductor tape-out, SilTerra/Luceda partnership, and four 

Fortune Global 500 customers in Stage 3 of the Design Win Cycle. Importantly, the Marvell-Polariton acquisition (closed 
February 2026,  ~$3.25B + $2.25B earnout)  creates a tantalizing but unconfirmed technical link — Polariton’s plasmonic-organic-hybrid demos historically used Perkinamine,  but Marvell has announced no LWLG license or supply agreement.  

Bulls are extrapolating; bears point out the company has been “near commercialization” since its PSI-TEC days in the 2000s.

text:
PO, CPO를 포함한 Photonics 섹터에서
PIC 설계, 제조사들은 200G/lane이상을 이야기하며 그 이상을 바라보지만

제대로된 EIC 200G/lane을 설계하고
그걸 Foundry로 만들 수 있는 기업은 한정적인데 사람들은 그걸 간과하고 있는듯하다.

Photonics는 PIC, EIC, DSP, LASER, PD Detector, 차세대 패키징이 한번에 가능해야 비로소 제대로 만들어졌다 할 수 있다.

PIC 200G를 시연에 성공했어도, PD Detector가 그걸 못받아 들이는 사양이면 무용지물이고, EIC가 신호를 제대로 못주면 그것 또한 의미 없는 것이다.

그 신호를 밀어줄 EIC/driver, 받아줄 PD/TIA, 보정할 DSP, 버틸 laser, 그리고 그걸 낮은 loss와 높은 yield로 묶는 패키징이 같이 맞아야 한다.

결국 Photonics는 부품 기술이 아니라, 시스템 통합 싸움이다.

text:
$AMD just reported a monster ER and sent every CPU stock to the moon. Up +15% after hours.

Since I started publicly sharing my portfolio I’ve been clear about where I’m putting my money and why:

The AI buildout: 

Photonics / Memory / CPUs / NeoClouds

So why CPUs and why now?

$AMD said it best on the call: in the past the CPU-to-GPU ratio was 1-to-4 or 1-to-8. Now it’s moving toward 1-to-1. And with agentic AI, you could end up with more CPUs than GPUs. That’s a structural shift.

Here’s how my CPU basket is doing since I entered:

$INTC (January) – entry at ~$55, bought the local top because I’m the worst trader ever. Now +105%.

$AMD (late March) – entry at ~$202, bought the local bottom because I’m the best trader ever. Now +76%.

$ARM (late March) –entry at $137 after the CPU for AI thesis started gaining traction. Now +62%.

If you think you’re late I can assure you you’re not. Not super early either. But agentic 

AI spending is just getting started and CPUs are in the critical path of every dollar spent.

Still bullish all CPU plays.

In a new blog, Joseph Chon explains why integrating MACsec directly into coherent DSPs inside ZR/ZR+ pluggable modules represents a meaningful shift in how scale-across networks are secured. Compared to embedding MACsec in switch ASICs or placing dedicated security devices on circuit boards, module-based MACsec reduces cost, complexity and deployment risk while making upgrades significantly easier. Marvell has already introduced several industry firsts enabling this approach, including the Electra 2nm 1.6T coherent DSP, the Libra 800G coherent DSP and the Aquila M coherent-lite DSP, all with integrated MACsec. By 2030, pluggables are projected to account for approximately 85% of scale-across ports shipped. text:
Threadguy explains why he slammed Zcash this weekend 

“Everybody in the stock market is so rich right now. Everybody is up so much money… there is just trillions of dollars that have been printed in the stock market”

“They’re ripping everything. We’re doing photonics now, everything’s an AI bottleneck… anything that could be remotely related to AI that hasn’t pumped, rip that, South Korea stocks now… rip that”

“When I saw the metals to full AI to South Korean stock rotation I’m like people are desperate for some reflexivity”

“There’s three assets in crypto that just haven’t moved Hyperliquid, Zcash and Bitcoin. Zcash is like an $8B coin… it’s worth nothing”

“I’m watching Google add $500B in a day and I’m saying to myself man they’re gonna pop it”

“Bitcoin, Zcash and Hyperliquid all of a sudden look good. I was bidding Zcash a little bit but then I just slammed over the weekend”

“Bitcoin, Zcash, Hyperliquid are the three set up… I just got the tingle”

text:
Photonics and memory are both getting re-rated right now because AI infrastructure got declared a national security priority. 

That changes the capital flow entirely. Government tailwind plus hyperscaler spend plus the CPO transition hitting simultaneously.

Optimistically you’re looking at 40-60% CAGR on photonics through 2028 with 

CPO as the accelerant. Memory, specifically HBM, is already running 50%+ growth and that probably holds or steepens as cluster density increases.

The Goldman re-rate just gave institutional cover for what was already obvious to anyone close to the hardware stack.

Everyone is looking for the next Nvidia.

• Photonics → $LITE $COHR $AAOI
• Networking → $MRVL $ANET  
• Memory → $MU $RMBS  
• Power → $VICR $NVTS $MPWR  
• Grid → $VRT $ETN $PWR

Many already partner with Nvidia directly.

Nvidia scales only if this stack scales.

That’s where the asymmetry is.

text:
$SITM my second blockbuster this season, after $STRL.
25% up on earnings!!!

I was a bit nervous because of the cost of Renesas acquisition in Feb. 

All the photonics chasers in X miss out this gem. I don't know how and why.

High precision clock is very important for synchronization in any digital communication -- between logic units (GPUs, CPUs, TPUs) for parallel computation and conversion of electrical signals to optical signals. That's right. There is a unit called DSP (digital signal processor) which takes binary electrical data from the logic units and transforms it into an electrical signal for light modulation unit which chops the CW light. For 800G/1.6T to work, it requires to handle sub-70 femtosecond jitter. Although $SWKS can achieve this, but in a convoluted way. $SITM's MEMS Resonators are tiny and work solid at 100C. Ideal for optical tranceivers and CPOs.

Another huge positive story is Renesas integration, where $SITM can vertically integrate this crucial part of the photonic fabrics. Instead of having a separate clock chip on a board, Renesas' microcontroller unit can have $SITM's clock integrated inside the package. With this, $SITM will be able to distribute the timing signal, clean jitters for 800G/1.6T networking, synchronize 5G/6G base stations. It will be unbeatable!
embedded_text:
$SITM is creating a very wide moat around high speed MEMS, I think.

EpiSeals, DualMEMS, plua 100+ patents owned by Luts
Now, adding all IPs from Renesas

Laser, Photonics, ... without ultrafast clocking doesn't make much sense for sure.

Catalyst-driven setup accelerating in AI silicon photonics. Pre-revenue, but de-risking fast with real foundry + IP momentum. Optical interconnect bottleneck in data centers is real — low-power, high-bandwidth EO polymers targeting 200G/400G+ lanes.

Major Recent Catalysts :

• April 29, 2026: Engaged Michael Best & Friedrich LLP (Am Law 200) as strategic IP advisor. Focus: invention harvesting, patent fortress, international filings, portfolio management & licensing/tech transfer to foundries. Clear commercialization push.

• March 11, 2026: Development Agreement with Tower Semiconductor — integrating Perkinamine® polymers into PH18 SiPho platform. Multiple engineering tapeouts planned for 2026 targeting high-speed, low-power modulators.

• March 16, 2026: High-speed modulator platform now live in GDSFactory PDK for GlobalFoundries. Plug-and-play for designers & hyperscalers.
Fundamentals Snapshot (transparent):

•  Pre-revenue (~$237K in 2025) with typical development-stage losses.
•  Debt-free + solid cash runway into 2027+ via recent raise/ATM.
•  Active ATM (normal for this stage — funding R&D/tapeouts).
•  Insider sales mostly tax/option exercises post-run (standard, not dumping).
•  ~9-10% short interest, elevated but manageable. No new red flags or scandals.

Risks vs Reward: Execution on 2026 tapeouts/design wins critical. Commercial revenue targeted ~2027. High-volatility micro-cap story stock in a massive AI tailwind (optical transceivers market → $100B by 2030).

But step back to a 5-year view and the stock is still down 28.99%. So this is a recovery, not a breakout to new highs.

So what's behind the move?
SOI wafers feed a broad set of end markets (silicon photonics, RF, and others).

Post 7
DYNASTY highlights the FORTH–NTNU joint workshop in Heraklion, where students, technicians and researchers come together around emerging photonic materials and processes.

#DYNASTYProject #FORTH #Photonics #ResearchTraining #HorizonEurope https://t.co/Re6cZENNGe

@OhioNakamoto @JonErlichman @VentureCoinist Photonics and optical networking look primed next in the AI data center buildout—Coherent, Fabrinet, and Arista Networks are frequently cited for big upside as hyperscalers scale. Power and efficiency plays could follow too. 

The rally's still early in some layers. DYOR. 🚀

text:
$SIVE $LPK $ALRIB $INFQ $LWLG 🔥
Here’s my conviction portfolio - I’ve invested the most in these five, because they sit at the epicenter of the exploding AI, photonics, quantum, and advanced manufacturing mega-trends

$SIVE – Sivers Semiconductors
Leading player in high-performance photonics lasers and mmWave wireless chips. Powering AI data centers, SATCOM, 5G/6G, and optical networks with game-changing efficiency. 

$LPK – LPKF Laser & Electronics
Precision laser systems for next-gen electronics manufacturing - PCB prototyping, laser welding, and advanced materials processing. The hidden enabler behind faster, cheaper, and more sustainable chip & component production.

$ALRIB – Riber
World leader in Molecular Beam Epitaxy (MBE) equipment - the critical tool for growing the compound semiconductor materials used in lasers, RF chips, VCSELs, and quantum devices.

$INFQ – Infleqtion
Pure-play quantum computing building neutral-atom quantum computers, precision sensors, quantum clocks, and cold-atom tech for defense, commercial, and government customers.

$LWLG – Lightwave Logic
Pioneering electro-optic polymers (Perkinamine) that deliver ultra-high-speed, low-power photonic chips. The polymer platform that could finally solve the bandwidth bottleneck in AI data centers and silicon photonics.

Did I missed something?

text:
The most interesting pattern in AI photonics this year is not a fundamental story. It's a flow story. And it's already played out twice in the last six weeks. Something that popped up when I was doing research on $SIVE for an article that drops tomorrow.

$SOI Soitec. March 26: Morgan Stanley upgrades with photonics SOI as the bull case. April 22: they cross the 5% threshold and now hold 6.50% of the company, acquired through an off-market transaction. The same window saw multiple disclosed shorts from Two Sigma, Numeric, AHL Partners, and JPMorgan Asset Management.

$IQE. Up 323% in twelve months. Stockopedia tags it a Momentum Trap, trading 283% above its 200-day moving average. April 27: $MTSI MACOM leads an 81 million GBP strategic investment that ends IQE's strategic review.

The setup is the same in both names.

European compound semiconductor companies trade cheap on their home exchanges because local investors do not price the AI photonics optionality. US institutional desks notice. Analyst reports flag overvaluation. Retail and local holders sell into the perceived weakness. US institutions accumulate the float at the bottom of the shake-out. The fundamentals are not the trigger. The flow is.

Now look at $SIVE Sivers Semiconductors. Up over 900% in twelve months. Just announced a 1.6T transceiver collaboration with Jabil. Evaluating a Nasdaq New York dual listing. Stockopedia tags it a Momentum Trap. Analyst consensus 79% below market price.

Identical setup to where Soitec was in early March.

I want to be careful here. This is a hypothesis based on three data points. It tells you what could happen if the playbook repeats. It does not tell you when. It does not tell you whether the stock takes a 50% drawdown first. The companies are in different stages of maturity. Soitec has 890M EUR in revenue. Sivers has 30M USD, 72% of it NRE.

But the playbook is real. The receipts are public. It explains why analyst targets 80% below market, Momentum Trap classifications, and off-market transactions keep clustering in the same sub-sector at the same time.

I do not own any of these names. My framework is fundamentals, and I do not have edge in predicting where Morgan Stanley parks a 6.5% position three months from now. But the flow case is the most interesting non-fundamental thesis I have seen in AI infrastructure picks-and-shovels this year.

Worth watching closely.

The AI Super Cycle rotates in phases

Semis like $ARM $AMD $INTC were Phase 1.

Money is already rotating:

Memory → $MU
Photonics / Optical → $NOK
Networking → $ANET
Compute → $IREN
Power → $VRT
Materials → $MP
Space → $ASTS
Defense / Drones → $KTOS $ONDS
Robotics → $TSLA

All you need to do is catch one part of one rotation of the cycle.

text:
No, Infleqtion and PsiQuantum are separate companies with different quantum tech approaches (neutral atoms vs. photonics).

Both are tenants at Chicago's Illinois Quantum & Microelectronics Park (IQMP). PsiQuantum is the anchor building a large photonic utility-scale system there. Infleqtion is developing its own neutral-atom quantum computer on the same campus as part of a $50M+ Illinois initiative. No direct affiliation between them.
@AIInvestorHQ Semiconductors and photonics are the future and there is nothing we can do about it

text:
Massive YTD so far for PHOTONICS: The next layer of the AI buildout.
AI isn't just a compute story anymore.
It's becoming a data movement problem.
And the market is starting to price that in.
Why photonics is moving
More GPUs → more nodes → more data moving between them.
Copper can't keep up. Too much heat, too much power, wrong distances.
Photonics is the fix. And the buildout is already underway.
What the data is showing
$AAOI revenue grew 34% YoY last quarter — not speculation, actual orders.
800G and 1.6T optical capacity is being expanded right now to meet hyperscaler demand.
Earnings this week are the tell
$LITE — May 5 (after close)
$COHR — May 6 (after close)
$AAOI — May 7
If guidance on 800G/1.6T demand holds or accelerates, this thesis gets legs into 2027.
If hyperscaler commentary disappoints — the chart above gets a reality check.
Watch the guidance, that will be the clue. 
#photonics

text:
Sivers Semiconductors ($SIVE) is a small-cap player with real momentum: FY2025 revenue SEK 304M (+25% YoY), $453M pipeline, and production ramps in AI photonics, LiDAR (potential $28-53M cumulative), and SATCOM/defense deals. It's 50x smaller than $LITE (~$68B mcap, $2.1B TTM rev) or $COHR (~$62B mcap, multi-billion scale).

That size difference creates higher torque if execution hits—but also higher risk: ongoing losses, execution hurdles, competition from scaled peers, and valuation sensitivity. The big caps are more mature with broader customer bases.

Data backs the growth case for SIVE, yet skeptics rightly note small-caps often fail to deliver. DYOR; this isn't advice.

"strategy of prioritizing bleeding-edge projects like photonics, nano-materials, and AI agents over saturated markets or outdated apps, believing it yields better long-term ROI and personal learning."  Yes, push the needle forward, we live in an age of disruption, go full-speed ahead on new exciting things, if it doesn't exist join or find like-minded people who can push the needle forward with you... Anduril is like one of those companies, and a few others that keep that startup/bright-eyed vibe instead of getting lost in corporate jargon.

We can push through so many hurdles right now and help others with disruptive tech! :)

text:
🚀 $SIVE Price Targets: Week / Month / Year (Top 3 Catalysts Driving It)

$SIVE (Sivers Semiconductors) has absolutely exploded — up ~815% YTD and trading at ~38 SEK after hitting a 52-week high of 44.70 SEK. This photonics/laser play (wireless, optical comms, LiDAR, CPO for AI data centers) is riding serious momentum. Here’s my take on realistic price targets based on the top 3 near-term catalysts:

1. Nasdaq New York Dual Listing (Biggest Near-Term Catalyst)
•  Announced April 16, 2026 — they’re doing the PCAOB audit uplift and delayed the 2025 annual report to May 15 to prep for it (while staying domiciled in Sweden).
•  This opens the floodgates to US tech capital, higher liquidity, and a potential valuation rerating (US multiples love photonics/AI plays).
•  Impact: Listing progress/news could be the spark.

2. Major LiDAR Ramp (Q4 2026 Production Start)
•  Strategic customer integrating Sivers’ lasers + optical amplifiers across multiple platforms.
•  Revenue potential: $53M–$138M cumulative over the product lifecycle (auto + industrial). Ramp begins Q4 2026.
•  This is real, high-volume revenue coming online.

3. CPO / AI Data Center Supercycle (Longer-Term Rocket Fuel)
•  Sivers is a leader in external laser sources for Co-Packaged Optics (CPO).
•  Recent Jabil collab on 1.6T LRO transceivers for AI data centers + 64% pipeline growth to $453M. Tied directly into the NVDA/AVGO optical boom.
My speculative price targets (NFA — this thing is volatile as hell, high P/S, execution risk):

•  Next Week: 40–45 SEK (Momentum carry + any listing/earnings buzz. Watch for pullbacks after the recent spike.)

•  Next Month: 50–65 SEK (Dual-listing updates + continued AI/photonics hype. Could test new highs if volume stays strong.)

•  Next Year (End 2026 / into 2027): 90–130+ SEK (Dual listing complete + LiDAR ramp kicking off + CPO orders scaling. Market cap could easily 2–3x from here on execution.)

This is a high-conviction, high-risk name — asymmetric upside if the catalysts hit, but it’s already priced in a ton of optimism. Fundamentals are still catching up (still loss-making), but the narrative is strong in the AI/optics supercycle.

Position size accordingly and DYOR. What’s your $SIVE target? 

Holding long or swinging the volatility? 🔥

(Prices in SEK. Current ~38 as of late April close. Not financial advice.)

text:
$AEVA is no longer just a LiDAR story.

The market is still valuing it like a speculative auto sensor company while management is quietly positioning for the next AI infrastructure wave:

• Silicon photonics
• Optical interconnects
• AI data center bandwidth bottlenecks
• Semiconductor manufacturing
• Defense autonomy
• Smart infrastructure
• Industrial robotics
This earnings call changed the narrative.
Key points the market is missing:
→ Nikon commercial deployment is LIVE
→ Shipping 1,000+ industrial sensors already
→ Semiconductor equipment customers using AEVA sensors in fabs
→ NVIDIA DRIVE Hyperion reference LiDAR outside China
→ Defense expansion with Forterra
→ Data center optical interconnect commentary now entering the story
The important part:
AEVA’s core competency isn’t “car LiDAR.”
It’s high-performance silicon photonics + FMCW sensing.
That has applications FAR beyond automotive.

The AI capex cycle is creating massive demand for:
• optical transport
• photonics
• machine perception
• edge AI sensing

And AEVA is now sitting at the intersection of all four.

Still early.
Still speculative.
But this is evolving from a niche EV sensor play into a broader Physical AI + AI photonics platform story.
That’s where rerating potential comes from.

Update on tonight's $COHR print:

Revenue: +20.5% YoY, beat by 1.7%
EPS: $1.41 (missed $1.43 by $0.02)
Q4 guide: $1.98B mid (in-line)
After-hours: -9%

That's THREE photonics names in 48 hours that beat and sold off:
$LITE -8%
$OUST -11%
$COHR -9%

Multiple compression isn't just on premium names anymore. It's the whole sector.

My framework update:

text:
My exposure to photonics 

*You've basically called all of them

Think these are positioned well to capture capex ramp over the next 3-5 years 

SIVE 
- Sivers Semiconductors (lasers for CPO optical engines)

OE Solutions  
- InP lasers + ELSFP modules for CPO

LPK (LPKF)    
- Laser systems for glass interposers & advanced photonics packaging

ALMU          
- Aeluma (compound semiconductor photonics / quantum dot lasers)

Episil Precision (https://t.co/TuTwLjmIHE) 
- Silicon photonics epi wafers

IQE 
- Compound semi epi wafers (InP/GaAs/GaN) for photonics

SOI (Soitec)  
- Engineered substrates (SOI) critical for silicon photonics

ShunShin (https://t.co/f1ZwK4ljHn) 
- Optical packaging & assembly for CPO

text:
$inv
Reposting again 

Everyone still has eyes on the memory and photonics  trade $SNDK $MU $DRAM $LITE $COHR $SIVE

Whilst there might be some more room in these still, they are becoming somewhat crowded. 

Exactly why Goldman is saying liquid cooling is the next trade like memory. 

Liquid cooling has lagged the overall data centre trade.

All the private cooling companies are getting hoovered up at the moment in big private deals. $ETN and Boyd. $ECL and Cool IT. $VRT and Strategic Thermal Labs. 
These big companies are trying to build out their end to end solutions for next gen cooling. 

Then you’ve got the Series B round of $jci in @AccelsiusATX $inv

It’s actually hard to get exposure to a public pure play cooling company right now. 
I think $inv is the closest way with there 43.2% holding in @AccelsiusATX 

From my research it’s abundantly clear the direction liquid cooling is going.

$inv
From the GS global equites call this AM

They expect liquid cooling to be the next bottle neck. Similar to memory last year $mu $sndk $5930.sk SK Hynix

A great example of this was: the memory theme that emerged late last year. The current bottlenecks now exist in fiber https://t.co/B6Ws7wS5lD

InP laser chokepoint + RF‑photonics flagship, with Glasgow’s constrained InP capacity, a SEK 304M FY25 revenue base, positive Q4 photonics EBITDA, and a $453M pipeline giving it four distinct growth vectors (LiDAR, CPO ELS, SATCOM/wireless, and telecom/defense/6G) where any single one could eventually rival today’s total business.

Still upside from here, we've got a MASSIVE research report coming Saturday on $SIVE.

A unique look that not many are exploring.

Will send here: https://t.co/lUESQXjyp9

text:
The Full Optical AI Industry Chain:  

1. IC Design & Manufacturing  
(Where the brains are built)

ASIC / xPU / Photonic ICs  
→ $NVDA $MRVL $AVGO $LITE $COHR $INTC $AMD $CSCO  

Wafer Foundries  
→ $TSM $TSEM $GFS $UMC  

2. Materials (The Overlooked Layer)  
(The foundation of photonics)  

Indium Phosphide  
→ $AXTI $IQE  

Gallium Arsenide  
→ $AXTI $MTSI  

This layer is underfollowed and could see strong upside as optical demand expands.

3. Optical Components  
(The data highways)  

VCSEL / EML Lasers  
→ $LITE $COHR $AVGO $AAOI  

Optical Module Assembly  
→ $FN $COHR $LITE $MRVL $CSCO  

Fiber / WDM / Optical Connectivity  
→ $CIEN $GLW $LITE  

MPO Connectors  
→ $GLW $APH  

Optical Fiber  
→ $GLW  

4. Packaging & Testing  
(The hidden backbone)  

Packaging  
→ $ASX $AMKR $FN $JBL  

Packaging Equipment  
→ $KLIC 

Testing  
→ $TER $KEYS $FORM $AEHR

As AI clusters scale into hundreds of thousands of GPUs, the challenge becomes moving data faster, farther, and with less power. Copper is hitting its limits. Optical is the next layer of scale. Why optical matters: • Higher bandwidth • Lower latency • Lower power consumption • Less heat • Better long-distance transmission That’s why hyperscalers are aggressively investing in optical-heavy infrastructure. text:
✅ قائمة المراقبة الدورية للـ AI (النسخة الكاملة)

يهذي الدورة الكبرى للذكاء الاصطناعي اللي لسى معظم الناس ما انتبهوا لها:

السيميكوندكترز (Semis): $NVDA $AMD $ARM $AVGO $MRVL $INTC $TSM $QCOM

الذاكرة (Memory): $MU $WDC $STX $SNDK

الفوتونيكس والألياف البصرية (Photonics / Optical): $LITE $COHR $AAOI $GLW $JBL $AXTI $LWLG $NOK

الشبكات (Networking): $ANET $CSCO $CIEN $JNPR

الحوسبة ومراكز البيانات (Compute / Data Centers): $IREN $CIFR $NBIS

الطاقة والشبكة الكهربائية (Power / Grid): $VRT $ETN $PWR $GEV $CEG $SMR $OKLO

المواد والمعادن (Materials / Minerals): $MP $LAC $UUUU $USAR

text:
$LWLG is poised for explosive growth with an unmatched competitive edge that positions the company as a clear leader in next-generation photonics for AI-driven data centers and high-speed communications. 

With over 70 issued U.S. and international patents plus dozens more pending, they enjoy complete bulletproof freedom-to-operate across their entire suite of electro-optic polymer materials and devices, eliminating any risk of IP challenges and safeguarding their path to market dominance. 

Their state-of-the-art in-house manufacturing operation right in Denver, Colorado, produces rare-earth-free, fully supply-chain-resilient polymers that are already scaled and ready for high-volume production, giving them total control over quality and eliminating the geopolitical vulnerabilities that plague competitors reliant on overseas critical materials. 

Now in 2026, the company has successfully completed its full transition from pure R&D into commercialization mode, laser-focused on delivering the exceptional reliability and thermal stability required for seamless integration into leading foundry processes like Silicon Photonics. 

At the core of this breakthrough are their proprietary Perkinamine polymers, which deliver superior electro-optic performance with blazing bandwidths beyond 200 Gbps per lane, dramatically lower power consumption, and industry-first reliability metrics that have passed rigorous Telcordia GR-468 testing. 

This powerful combination of ironclad intellectual property, domestic manufacturing independence, and polymers engineered for real-world foundry deployment creates a formidable moat that will drive significant market penetration and shareholder value as demand for ultra-efficient optical interconnects surges in the AI era.

text:
The optics bottleneck thesis is no longer fringe. It’s becoming one of the clearest AI infrastructure pressure points. LightCounting has the AI-cluster optical interconnect market moving from ~$5B in 2024 to $10B+ by 2026. Silicon photonics chip sales projected from ~$0.8B in 2023 to $3B+ by 2029.

Cignal AI had 2025 datacom optical components growing 60%+ to over $16B, with 800G transceiver shipments roughly doubling YoY and 1.6T entering production for Nvidia in 2026.

Dell’Oro sees 2026 as the first real volume year for 1.6T switches, with the ramp potentially passing 5M ports within 1–2 years, faster than 800G.

The demand backdrop is pretty obvious: Meta + Corning signed a multi-year agreement worth up to $6B to accelerate hyperscale data center buildout.

Amazon guided to ~$200B in 2026 capex. Amazon + Anthropic added another $25B commitment in April 2026. Lumentum’s CEO has framed hyperscaler AI infrastructure spend around ~$700B for 2025.

The key point: AI capex is not just GPUs. The cluster has to be connected. More compute means more optical links, more 800G/1.6T demand, more pressure on supply chains. But market structure matters.

Supply-chain reporting suggests Nvidia’s incremental 800G optical module orders are still mainly captured by InnoLight and Eoptolink, with the rest split across Coherent, Lumentum, Broadcom, and other US vendors. So for names like $AAOI, the bull case is not “market leader tomorrow.”

It’s more specific: A smaller US-onshore alternative sitting inside a real bottleneck, where even modest share gains, reshoring preference, or customer diversification can matter if the optics cycle keeps expanding. The thesis is real.
The debate now is execution, share capture, margin durability, and whether the market is underpricing the smaller beneficiaries.

embedded_text:
It's interesting how the people most worried about the SOH/energy crisis are also most skeptical of AI.

On the other hand, the most AI-pilled are dismissive of the SOH.

It's all a big Rorschach test...

text:
🚀 El sector de Ópticas / Photonics lidera las subidas hoy:

•  $VIAV +15.95%
•  $LWLG +12.84%
•  $AXTI +10.88%
•  $CIEN +9.49%
•  $AEHR +9.48%
•  $GLW +7.21%
•  $LITE +6.72%
•  $POET +6.67%
•  $AAOI +5.22%
•  $COHR +4.22% 
Todas estas compañías forman parte del superciclo de photonics: la infraestructura óptica crítica para los data centers de IA (transceivers de alta velocidad, láseres InP, silicon photonics y testing).

Nota: $AXTI publica resultados esta noche después del cierre (EPS esperado: -0,07$; Ingresos ~26,2 Mn$).

El momentum en la cadena de suministro óptica de la IA sigue intacto.

¿Qué opinas? ¿Continuará el rally o es solo rotación?

text:
$AEHR It's been interesting to watch the stock the past week, following the pullback which $POET kicked off in the silicon photonics space.

$TER reported on Tuesday. Great numbers, but the stock dropped by double digits yesterday; a drop that $AEHR didn't participate much in. Teradyne is up again today.

Is the stock ahead of itself? Yes, but it's tough to fade this rally. The acceleration is clear, and it sounds like there's a strong chance of additional orders landing in May per the CEO. How much of that is priced in? We'll find out next month.

text:
วันนี้กลุ่ม photonics ขึ้นกันยับๆ เลย

$AAOI กับ $LITE วิ่งพร้อมกันแบบนี้แปลว่า ตลาดกำลังมองว่า “ของจะเริ่มไม่พอ” และ CEO $LITE ก็บอกว่าของขาดถึง 2027 เลย 

แล้วตอนนี้ผมเริ่มเห็น photonics กำลังขยับจากเป็นแค่ component ไปเป็น platform (system) ที่ใครคุม architecture ก็จะได้กิน margin มากขึ้น

แล้วคอขวดถัดไปก็ไม่ใช่ bandwidth แล้ว แต่คือ integration ว่าจะเอาของทั้งหมดไปประกอบเป็นระบบให้ใช้งานจริงยังไง ซึ่งเป็นจุดที่พวก system player อย่าง $CIEN จะเริ่มเข้ามามีบทบาทมากขึ้น

สรุปตอนนี้คือ “ใครมีของ + คุม system ได้” ก็จะอิ่มมากกว่า

Long list but concentration varies wildly across these categories.

text:
$SIVE / $SIVEF CEO said this in February. 

Hard not to be bullish 📈📈

text:
You guys know you can invest in the stock market without Serenity’s approval, right?

He already mapped out the hottest segment of 2026-2030 (Photonics), the sub-segments (lasers, packaging, testing, substrates…) and even the adjacent beneficiaries, yet it is never enough.

You will grow as retail investor if you follow your own DD and learn from it, even if it doesn’t play out. 

$SMOP, $NOK, $MXL, $M7U, $TRT, $ASOL, $LWLG and hundreds of others are doing just extraordinary.

Just go with the flow of AI / Photonics / Memory / NeoClouds… you can hardly miss with a few exceptions (I’m looking at you, $POET).

Most if not all will continue to go up – maybe not $SIVE up 500% in a month, but 100% in a 1-3 months, which is incredible in and of itself.

Si la demande en bande passante pour les data centers AI continue d’exploser, les composants optiques restent importants. 2. $MXL - Petit semi avec exposition data centers, connectivité et possible optionalité AI infrastructure. 3. $VECO - Exposition aux équipements semi, surtout autour des matériaux avancés, compound semis et photonics manufacturing. 4. $AXTI - Play upstream photonics/materials. Les substrats peuvent devenir un bottleneck si la demande optique continue d’augmenter. Un des meilleurs long de l'année. 5. $IQE - Un des fournisseurs occidentaux d’epi-wafers les plus intéressants. Très risqué, mais potentiellement critique plus upstream dans les supply chains photonics. 6. $RPI - Raspberry Pi pourrait bénéficier si les agents AI locaux, OpenClaw/Picoclaw et les déploiements edge low-cost deviennent un vrai moteur de demande. Au vue de l'augmentation du compute dut à l'agentic et l'explosion des Mac Mini, play ultra smart en AI Local. 7. $EXA- Drones sous marins, souveraineté FR sous la mer. EXAIL a été largement repriced récemment mais reste un narratif de dingue. 8. $ALKAL - Kalray est intéressant comme play européen sur edge AI / data processing infrastructure. Très spéculatif, mais cohérent avec le thème AI compute. L'exposition aux DPU et à la souveraineté européenne en fait un acteur inratable. 9. $ALRIB - Riber donne une exposition aux équipements MBE, importants pour compound semis, photonics et matériaux avancés. Deal avec $MSFT potentiel. 10. $AMD - Une des principales alternatives à Nvidia sur GPUs, CPUs et AI compute hyperscaler. L'agentic est entrain re-rate la demande en CPU, ça tombe bien, AMD fait les deux et même des DPU. 11. $SK Hynix - La mémoire reste un des bottlenecks les plus évidents de l’AI infrastructure, surtout avec la demande HBM. 11 bis. $SK Square - Holding coréenne avec une exposition importante à SK Hynix. Pertinent comme manière indirecte de jouer la demande HBM, mémoire AI et le re-rating potentiel de l’écosystème semi coréen. 12. $SNDK - Exposition storage / NAND. Si la création de données liée à l’AI continue d’exploser, le stockage reste structurellement important. 13. $ROBOTIS - Nom coréen exposé à la robotique et à l’automation. Si le thème robotics continue de se diffuser globalement ça pourrait continuer à grimper fort, surtout après l'ouverture aux capitaux US/EU 14. $VPG - Exposition capteurs pour robotique, automation et systèmes industriels. Pas évident, mais potentiellement important si le physical AI se développe. 15. $GOOGL - Gemini, YouTube, TPUs, Waymo, cloud et énorme operating income pour financer l’AI buildout. Pour moi vrai capteur de la rentabilité de l'IA et la plus à même de la rentabiliser. 16. $QBTS - Exposition quantum computing. Très spéculatif, mais fait partie des noms publics surveillés quand le thème quantum revient. 17. $INFQ - Infleqtion donne une exposition quantum via neutral atom computing, sensing et timing. Risqué, mais intéressant comme nouveau nom public du secteur. 18. $STMPA - Exposition européenne aux semiconducteurs via automotive, industrial, power et embedded chips. Retournement haussier poussée par l'exposition à la photonique silicium avec les PIC100. 19. $NVTS - Exposition power semis / GaN. Si AI, EVs, data centers et efficacité énergétique restent des thèmes clés, ce secteur reste intéressant. 20. $NBIS - Nom AI cloud / infrastructure. Risqué, mais potentiellement un des plays non-megacap les plus intéressants sur la demande AI compute. 21. $COHR - Play photonics/optical infrastructure plus établi. Intéressant si les data centers AI continuent d’augmenter leurs besoins en transceivers, lasers et composants optiques. 22. $LITE - Exposition directe à l’optical cycle, notamment via lasers, photonics et potentielle supply chain TPU/hyperscaler. Plus clean que les moonshots upstream décrit précedemment. 23. $2CRSI - Petit play français sur serveurs haute performance / AI infrastructure. Très spéculatif, mais intéressant si la demande européenne pour compute, HPC et data centers continue d’accélérer. Tout n'est pas à acheter aujourd'hui, faites vos recherches."}}}, "rest_id": "2050981295820550448", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "137701", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2051017744418582663", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "1126", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Nvidia just poured $4 billion into photonics companies. That's not a portfolio diversification move. It's a confession.

The photonics bet tells you Nvidia thinks the next bottleneck for AI scaling isn't compute. It's data movement.

GPU clusters scale by passing data between thousands of chips at insane speeds. Copper interconnects are hitting physical limits. The next leap requires light, optical interconnects, fiber inside the chip package, and laser-based data transmission across racks.

Whoever owns the optical layer wins the next AI capex cycle.

The names Jason Luongo flagged are the cleanest pure-plays. $LITE Lumentum makes the lasers, with a $2 billion commitment from Nvidia and a new 240,000 sq ft InP fab in North Carolina. $MRVL Marvell owns the optical DSP layer, the brains that encode and decode data through photonic links. $GLW Corning supplies the fiber and just signed a $6 billion multiyear deal with Meta for data center buildout.

$TSM TSMC is doing co-packaged optics through its COUPE platform. $AAOI Applied Optoelectronics has $124 million in 800G orders and over $200 million in 1.6T volume orders. $SOI Soitec has a 95 percent monopoly on photonics-grade silicon-on-insulator wafers.

The bull case across all six. AI capex isn't slowing. Hyperscalers are signing multi-year contracts. Pure-play photonics names are early in the adoption curve.

The bear case. Photonics is a deeply technical field with multi-year design cycles. Any execution delay or competitive threat hits valuations hard.

Asymmetric framing. Nvidia spent $4 billion of its own balance sheet because the math told them to.

Retail can't out-research Jensen Huang. Following the capex flow is the cleanest way to position behind the same trade.

Watch the photonics basket through 2027.

text:
Gustavo is a great example of why the bull market is over.

We do not have a bullish market but one bullish sector. If you do not own memory, photonics or - less likely for FinX, defensive names

You are underperforming.

This is why following strength and spotting names like $SOI or $SILC early enough, just after they give their first confirmations but not so lat that they gave all the returns, is so important.

Stocks' Strength > Convictions.

All due respect to Gustavo who I do not even know, his post came to my feed so I thought I could use it, but I know many are in the same boat, while not sharing it.

text:
Optical bottleneck is becoming impossible to ignore.

AI scaling is about moving data fast enough between GPUs, racks and clusters.

That is why photonics, CPO and optical interconnects matter.

Winners may be the component suppliers sitting quietly upstream of the entire AI infrastructure stack.

text:
The AI optics industry refers to the intersection of artificial intelligence and optics/photonics. It encompasses two main areas, though the term often highlights the high-growth tech infrastructure side in business and investment contexts.
1. AI-Powered Optical Systems in Healthcare & Diagnostics (Specialized/Niche)
Companies apply AI to optical imaging hardware for faster, more accessible diagnostics.

Key Example: AI Optics, Inc. develops portable retinal cameras (e.g., Sentinel Camera, FDA-cleared) with on-device AI to screen for diseases like diabetic retinopathy, AMD, and glaucoma at point-of-care (primary care, retail clinics) instead of specialist offices.
Broader Applications: AI assists in eyecare (e.g., detecting glaucoma/dry eye), lens manufacturing, frame design, and personalized eyewear via The Vision Council and others.

This segment improves access, speed, and accuracy in vision health but is smaller-scale compared to the tech side.
2. Photonics & Optical Components for AI Infrastructure (The Booming Market)
This is the dominant and fastest-growing interpretation. It involves using light (photons) instead of electricity (electrons/copper) for data movement in AI data centers, where massive GPU clusters create bottlenecks in speed, power, and heat.
Why it matters for AI:

AI training/inference requires enormous bandwidth between thousands of GPUs.
Traditional copper interconnects hit limits on distance, power consumption, and density.
Optics/photonics enable higher speed, lower power, lower latency, and better scalability via fiber optics, lasers, silicon photonics, and co-packaged optics (CPO).

Market Size & Growth:

AI optics market (transceivers, lasers, switches, etc.) projected to reach ~$90 billion by 2030, with strong double-digit CAGR. AI cluster optics alone grew rapidly (e.g., $16.5B in 2025 estimates).
Driven by hyperscalers (NVIDIA ecosystems, Google, etc.) and the shift to optical interconnects.

Key Technologies:

Optical transceivers and pluggables.
Silicon photonics (integrating optics on chips).
Co-packaged optics (CPO) and near-packaged optics.
Optical circuit switches (OCS).
Photonic chips for energy-efficient AI computing.

Major Players:

Broadcom, Cisco, Intel, Marvell.
Lumentum, Coherent, Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI).
Others in lasers, PICs (photonic integrated circuits), and manufacturing.

This segment is often called the "picks and shovels" of the AI boom — essential infrastructure enabling larger, more efficient AI clusters.
Overall Outlook
The industry blends traditional optics (lenses, imaging) with cutting-edge photonics. The photonics-for-AI side is exploding due to data center demands, while AI-in-optics (design, diagnostics, manufacturing) is steadily advancing across sectors. Growth is fueled by AI scaling needs, 5G/6G, and Industry 4.0.

Photonic chips for energy-efficient AI computing. Major Players: Broadcom, Cisco, Intel, Marvell. Lumentum, Coherent, Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI). Others in lasers, PICs (photonic integrated circuits), and manufacturing. This segment is often called the "picks and shovels" of the AI boom — essential infrastructure enabling larger, more efficient AI clusters. Overall Outlook The industry blends traditional optics (lenses, imaging) with cutting-edge photonics. The photonics-for-AI side is exploding due to data center demands, while AI-in-optics (design, diagnostics, manufacturing) is steadily advancing across sectors. Growth is fueled by AI scaling needs, 5G/6G, and Industry 4.0."}}}, "rest_id": "2050607707963490731", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "20", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Rothschild Redburn report on $CRDO ..

 While they rate $CRDO a Buy w/ PT $206, and correctly point out the photonics upside,  they definitely undershoot the upside on AEC assumptions

The bull case rebuts a binary market view on AEC erosion. The pivot from AEC to optical hinges on sustained near-term AEC growth while the optical franchise develops, then a lucrative optical business offsetting AEC deceleration later.

Catalysts:

AEC durability levers — 400G AEC reach extension, lower-bandwidth network penetration, server backplane deployments.

ZeroFlap optical transceivers entering a feverish growth market, with embedded PILOT telemetry software as a defensible differentiator vs commodity transceivers.

DustPhotonics acquisition opens a $6bn silicon photonics TAM, strengthening post-pivot optical positioning.

Forecast delta: Revenue +15–21% above consensus FY27/FY28; operating profit +23% ahead. Trading 16x FY28 non-GAAP EV/EBITDA.

Risks include :  ZF Optics conversion of all three qualifying hyperscaler customers by FY28 — execution risk is real. 

AEC duration assumes 400G reach extension succeeds; physics may not cooperate. 

DustPhotonics integration adds operational risk. Multiple re-rate to 22-24x demands optical mix proof, not promise. 

Customer concentration still 86% top-3.
